_comment: Phase 0 scoring-snapshot fixtures. Each entry freezes the scoring-critical fields of one scored product so we can detect drift across pipeline runs (especially during Phase 3 enricher canonical_id work). See docs/HANDOFF_2026-04-20_PIPELINE_REFACTOR.md § Phase 0.

fixture_schema.excluded_rationale: Percentiles recompute dataset-wide and drift with brand additions. Scoring metadata carries timestamps. Match ledger is a diagnostic trail. None of these affect user-visible correctness when the score_80 and verdict are stable.

changelog.notes: The prior baselines (last frozen at e2583594, the v3-era completion merge) were captured under the retired v3 /80 scorer. Stage 3 has since cut over to the v4 six-pillar producer (refactor f3bed2f2), so EVERY fixture drifted identically and structurally: output_schema_version 3.6.0->4.0.0, score_basis bioactives_scored->v4_six_pillar, evaluation_stage scoring->scored_v4, and the v3-only fields score_80/section_scores/breakdown/supp_type dropped (they do not exist in v4 output). This is the intended, already-shipped v3->v4 cutover, not a scoring regression: v4 is the production scorer (AGENTS.md). Re-frozen all 30 from the current v4 scored output. Added quality_score_v4_100 and quality_score_status to frozen_fields so the gate now pins the canonical v4 score, not only its score_100_equivalent compat mirror. Representative shifts (v3->v4, all shipped v4 numbers): Thorne Vitamin D 72.1->95.0, Iron Bisglycinate 69.2->90.5, Zinc 71.1->86.3 (v4 rewards clean single-ingredient forms); Thorne Catalyte 83.8->71.8, GoL Perfect Food 66.0->50.8 (v4 blend/transparency penalties). Two safety-relevant verdict moves reviewed: 306381 Nutricost PRE SAFE->CAUTION (STIMULANT_CAFFEINE_HIGH_DOSE, correct), 298070 Thorne FloraMend SAFE->POOR (v4 tier label, not a safety block).

Initial Phase 0 snapshot. Frozen from scored/ output on main @ uncommitted Period B changes. Silybin Phytosome score_80=51.8 and Phosphorus products are the Phase 3 canonical_id authoritative fix targets; their scores WILL change after Phase 3 and fixtures will be regenerated with a reviewed changelog entry.

sprints_covered: D4.3 B7 UL canonical-level aggregation (teratogenicity safety)
changelog.notes: Accumulated drift from Sprint D1→D4.3+D2.9 medical-grade accuracy work. All 15 products re-frozen against post-sprint-D scored output. Drift categories: B1 penalty shifts (banned/recalled re-classification), B3 dose adequacy (canonical dedup changes contribution math), A1/A2 ingredient quality (new aliases route previously-unmapped rows through proper IQM forms), B7 UL aggregation (same-canonical doses now summed for safety threshold check — Vitamin A teratogenicity fix). All drifts verified as legitimate scoring improvements, not regressions.

sprints_covered: D4.3 B7 UL teratogenicity aggregation now firing on multi-form UL products
D5.4 Dr Pham safety_warning / ban_context propagation
changelog.notes: CVS Spectravite (12012): B7_dose_safety_evidence LEN 0->1, B7_penalty 0->2, score_80 31.0->29.0. Thorne Iron Citrate (15805): similar B7 activation. Drift is the intended result of flipping collect_rda_ul_data to true — the scorer now actually sees safety_flags on products that exceed 150%+ UL, and the D4.3 teratogenicity aggregation catches multi-form doses that individually sit under UL but summed exceed it. All drifts verified as medically-correct: products receive a B7 penalty when they expose the consumer to OVER-UL dosing. Fixtures re-frozen against post-D5.2 scored output.

sprints_covered: E1.3.2.c probiotic CFU adequacy point uplift (config-driven)
E1.3.3 fish oil EPA/DHA parent-mass fallback (config-driven)
changelog.notes: Accumulated scoring drift from Sprint E1.3.x ingredient-quality refinements. Drift categories: A1 ingredient quality (enzyme recognition credit, fish oil parent-mass fallback), supp_type reclassification (multivitamin vs specialty), grade boundary shifts (Fair vs Below Avg). All drifts verified as legitimate scoring improvements from authored Sprint E1.3.x work, NOT regressions from the goal-matching contract refactor. The schema v6.0.0 goal-matching changes (compute_goal_matches refactor, Solution A alias map, Solution B product-name fallback) were verified scoring-neutral by isolating synthetic ingredients to the cluster-matching path only (no mutation of activeIngredients). Goal_matches and goal_match_confidence are not in the frozen_fields whitelist, so this changelog entry covers the pre-existing E1.3.x scoring drift only.

sprints_covered: E1.19 enrichment cluster-match hardening (ingredient dedup + min-dose inheritance + abbrev matching + qty>0 gate)
changelog.notes: A5c synergy-bonus dropped from 0.75 to 0.0 on two single-ingredient products (223319 Nutricost KSM-66, 15756 Thorne Vitamin D). Root cause: the previous enricher counted the same product ingredient twice when it substring-matched multiple cluster variants (e.g. ashwagandha matching both 'ashwagandha' and 'ksm-66 ashwagandha' as TWO hits), spuriously crossing match_count>=2 and earning the synergy bonus. E1.19's ingredient-dedup fix correctly counts one hit per product ingredient per cluster. Result: single-ingredient products no longer collect an undeserved synergy bonus. This is a clinically-correct drift — genuine synergy requires ACTUAL multi-ingredient coordination.

B37 — D7 category_error_type enum (41 forms tagged)

changelog.author: Pipeline + Dr Pham clinical review

sprints_covered: E1.21 compute_goal_matches score formula — MAX-of-required-weights (commit c9c1719)
changelog.notes: CVS Omega-3 100 mg (18141): Section A1 12.06 → 12.19, Section C added omega-3 fatty acids ingredient_points 3.98, score_80 47.9 → 48.0, grade Below Avg → Fair. Drift is the intended result of the goal_matches scoring refactor (Sprint E1.21) which switched to MAX-of-required-weights, allowing fish-oil products to surface omega-3 evidence credit they previously missed. Verified as a legitimate scoring improvement, not a regression.

changelog.change: Re-froze snapshot after adding bromelain IQM entry. Athletic Pure Pack contains bromelain; previously bromelain fell through to unspecified-form, now scores at bio_score=10. New entry caused +0.03 on A1 (quality) and unrelated +3.98 evidence point pickup on omega-3.

Re-froze all 26 contract snapshots after the v3.5 scoring overhaul (commit f0a1d05) shipped without snapshot updates. Drift signature: uniform +20-25% on section C ingredient_points + top_n_applied 3→4. Root cause: top_n_weights changed from [1.0, 0.5, 0.25] to [1.0, 0.7, 0.5, 0.3] (softer diminishing returns, rewards 4-ingredient targeted formulations). Two products went from blocked-state (0.0) to scored after upstream B-section fixes (Garden of Life Adrenal Daily Balance, Nutricost PRE Pre-Workout Complex).

Re-froze all 30 contract snapshots after the omega-3 bonus cap clinician decision (commit 7ceac0d) which redistributed Section E bands and capped omega3_dose_bonus.max at 2.0 (was 3.0). Drift signature: uniform section_scores.E_dose_adequacy.max: 3.0 → 2.0 across every product whether omega-3 or not (the field is in every scored output). Verified as the intended downstream effect of the omega-3 cap change. Also picks up minor v1.5.0 form-vocab refactor side effects via the cleaner regex expansion (Vitamin A Palmitate now extracting retinyl palmitate directly into forms[] surfaces a more accurate bio_score for products previously matching unspecified parent).

changelog.product_ids: 16 products added — coverage refresh + new archetypes
changelog.change: Registered 16 contract-snapshot fixtures that had been committed in 795fb00a (DV-backed dose correction bundle) but never added to the manifest. These are current-shipped refreshes of existing archetypes (Korean ginseng, Calcium Magnesium, CoQ10, Zinc 15, Vitamin D, KSM-66, Catalyte, Plant Collagen, FloraMend, Green Tea Phytosome, AAKG, Iron Bisglycinate, Joint Health) plus three new archetypes (Garden Raw Organic Perfect Food Original, Garden Baby Multivitamin Liquid pediatric CAUTION, Thorne Boswellia Phytosome). No scoring drift in this entry — pure registration of pre-existing fixtures so the manifest contract test passes.

sprints_covered: Fresh full-pipeline rerun after scoring-input performance fix
changelog.notes: Re-froze two legacy scoring snapshots after the full pipeline regenerated scored outputs from current taxonomy. Garden of Life Adrenal Daily Balance now uses canonical primary_type=multivitamin instead of stale b_complex, with a small A1/raw-score drift from that reclassification. Nutricost PRE Pre-Workout Complex now uses canonical primary_type=pre_workout instead of stale b_complex. Both refreshed values match the current enriched taxonomy and product identity; this snapshot update is separate from the v4 public-cap reconciliation fix.

Re-froze CVS Spectravite Advanced Formula (12012) after the UL-gate fix removed a false OVER_UL_Boron flag. Boron 0.4 mg is 2% of the 20 mg adult UL, so the B7 dose-safety penalty (2.0) was never warranted; removing it raised section B 14.5->16.5, quality_raw 48.82->50.82, and score_100_equivalent 61.0->63.5. This is a scoring correction, not a regression.

sprints_covered: P80 authority-citation and alias-precision audit (commit c92cada)
changelog.notes: Re-froze four snapshots after the reviewed Polysorbate 80 audit corrected the safety evidence and removed its ambiguous bare 'polysorbate' alias. CVS Spectravite (12012) retains its actual P80 match but receives the reviewed moderate B1 penalty (8.0->7.5). The three Pure Encapsulations products lose an unsupported 0.5-point B1 penalty because a non-P80 polysorbate must not inherit P80's evidence. These changes improve match specificity; they do not suppress a verified P80 safety signal.

Re-froze two May fixtures after the fresh pipeline materialized the existing low-severity inactive-additive data. Spring Valley Korean Panax Ginseng (239741) now has three low-tier processing-aid penalties totaling 1.5 rather than stale 2.0. Garden of Life Wobenzym N Joint Health (321405) now has one low-tier stearic-acid penalty of 0.5 rather than stale 1.0. The current reference data and B1 risk map already carried these low tiers when the prior fixtures were frozen; this is a stale-artifact correction, not a new clinical downgrade. Both products remain SAFE with the same grade.

sprints_covered: Canonical identity specificity and explicit-form preservation
Exact probiotic strain-code matching and CFU provenance
Probiotic blend/header deduplication and taxonomy routing
changelog.notes: Re-froze only the six products whose post-fix drift was reviewed against freshly regenerated brand outputs. Nature Made Ginkgo (180417) now retains its IQM-backed ginkgo active instead of being incorrectly stamped recognized_non_scorable. Vitamin Code Men (288344) and Raw Organic Perfect Food (241353) preserve the reviewed moderate clinical-support metadata rather than overstating it as high; scores are unchanged. Adrenal Daily Balance (204571) removes one false strain-specific clinical match and carries a 0.05 A1 calibration change. Plant Collagen Builder (274498) retains its explicit beta-carotene child identity instead of being collapsed into parent Vitamin A; the remaining score change is 47.0 to 46.9 from current IQM calibration. FloraMend (298070) is correctly routed as a probiotic using its label-derived 5 billion live cells at expiration, three disclosed strains, zero unsupported clinical-strain claims, and one physical proprietary blend; score_80 changes 30.9 to 34.0. Athletic Pure Pack (182730) and Thorne Green Tea (336337) returned exactly to their prior snapshots after the identity root fix and were deliberately not refrozen.

Re-froze 17 fixtures after a fresh full 28-brand pipeline run. All 28 brands passed; the other 21 brands produced zero drift, so this is the complete delta set. Each line was reviewed; none are regressions. (1) B5 BLEND DETECTION (9 products: 182730, 288344, 18141, 204571, 241353, 274498, 327416, +): the detector now resolves real named blends with their declared totals and children instead of collapsing them into an anonymous 'General Proprietary Blends' bucket with blend_total_mg=null penalized at the harshest 'none' tier. disclosure_tier now reflects actual label disclosure (none/partial/full) and impact_source moves count_share->mg_share. Net effect is penalty reduction where the label genuinely discloses: 18141 CVS Omega-3 blend is fully disclosed (alpha linolenic acid 50mg of 300mg) -> B5 2.0->0.0; 204571 Organic Adrenal Blend 850mg fully disclosed -> 3.25->0.0. Accuracy correction, not a loosening. (2) A1 MULTIVITAMIN/PRENATAL TRUE-FLOOR (6 products land on 10.8: 288344, 239600, 204571, 241353, 274498, 327416): A1 now uses max(avg, floor) per the SCORING_LOGIC 'floor' spec rather than 0.7*avg+0.3*floor. The repeated 10.8 is the floor. (3) A3 DELIVERY EVIDENCE (18141 1.0->0.0, 321969 2.0->0.0): delivery matches sourced from internal reference notes are no longer trusted; only label-authored evidence (name/fullName, active-ingredient rows, typed DSLD statements) counts. Verified 'bisglycinate' is NOT a listed term in enhanced_delivery.json, so Thorne Iron Bisglycinate (321969) loses previously false-positive tier-2 credit (grade Good->Fair). FOLLOW-UP: enhanced_delivery.json has a genuine vocabulary gap for amino-acid chelates (bisglycinate/glycinate) - the code is correct, the delivery data is incomplete. (4) ROUNDING (293943 score_80 51.5->51.6 only): Python round() is banker's rounding, so 31.95 -> 31.9 while the display showed 32.0 - display and verdict contradicted each other. Now Decimal ROUND_HALF_UP for both consistently. (5) A5/A5b STANDARDIZATION (245156 Goli 1.0->4.0 / 0.0->3.0): standardization credit restored. (6) COSMETIC (298070): activeIngredients[1]->[0] source_path/fingerprint shift from preserving linked display-only blend members; no score change, FloraMend's earlier SAFE->POOR regression is resolved.
